tool_args.label: Results are preliminary and subject to audit review

tool_args.label: Revenue variance drivers
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $126.6 million was $4.0 million lower than the Adopted Budget. This reflects lower capital reimbursements partially offset by higher ridership, stations, and advertising revenue.

payload.claim_text: Total revenue of $126.6 million was $4.0 million lower than the Adopted Budget. This reflects lower capital reimbursements partially offset by higher ridership, stations, and advertising revenue.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: Expense variance drivers
tool_args.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $301.8 million were $5.3 million or 1.8% unfavorable to the Budget. The primary drivers of this unfavorable variance include higher non-reimbursable labor expenses partially offset by the timing of capital expenditures and lower electric costs.

payload.claim_text: The primary drivers of this unfavorable variance include higher non-reimbursable labor expenses partially offset by the timing of capital expenditures and lower electric costs.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: Labor expense variance details
tool_args.raw_text: Payroll expenses were $8.1 million unfavorable to the Budget, reflecting lower capital project activity, increased hiring levels, lower attrition, the timing of retiree payouts, and employee retention payments.

payload.claim_text: Payroll expenses were $8.1 million unfavorable to the Budget, reflecting lower capital project activity, increased hiring levels, lower attrition, the timing of retiree payouts, and employee retention payments.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: Overtime expense drivers
tool_args.raw_text: Overtime expenses were $4.9 million unfavorable to the Budget primarily due to coverage required for must fill open positions.

payload.claim_text: Overtime expenses were $4.9 million unfavorable to the Budget primarily due to coverage required for must fill open positions.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: Electric Power favorable variance
tool_args.raw_text: Electric Power costs were $6.7 million favorable to the Budget due to lower rates.

payload.claim_text: Electric Power costs were $6.7 million favorable to the Budget due to lower rates.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad

tool_args.label: Headcount variance notes

payload.claim_text: (A) Variance reflects higher attrition than planned (B) Variance reflects delayed hiring of vacant positions (C) Budget adjustment to reflect realistic monthly growth of approximately 20 Full-Time Equivalents

tool_args.label: Performance indicator calculation notes
tool_args.raw_text: Monthly Performance Indicators include both East and West of Hudson revenue and expenses. The Standard Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators reflect MTA-wide adopted calculations. Adjusted indicators have been adjusted for comparability between Metro-North and the LIRR.

payload.claim_text: Monthly Performance Indicators include both East and West of Hudson revenue and expenses. The Standard Farebox Operating Ratio and Cost Per Passenger indicators reflect MTA-wide adopted calculations that exclude non-cash liability adjustments as well as the NHL share of MTA Police, Business Service Center and IT costs. Adjusted indicators have been adjusted for comparability between Metro-North and the LIRR and include Other Operating Revenue, removal of OPEB retiree expenses, and estimated farebox revenue from an equalization of the Connecticut fare structure.
